skill.name: xlsx
skill.description: Create, inspect, edit, clean, validate, and convert spreadsheet files. The skill focuses on Excel workbooks and tabular files, with guidance for formulas, formatting, and recalculation.
skill.summary: Work with Excel and tabular files using reliable spreadsheet workflows.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: The skill contains legitimate local spreadsheet and Office document tooling, including ZIP archive handling, temporary files, and subprocess calls to LibreOffice, gcc, git, and timeout utilities. Most static network, blocker, sensitive, obfuscation, and documentation findings are false positives from OOXML schema names, Markdown examples, or validation terminology. No prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed skill files.

remediation.issue: Office archive extraction uses extractall on user-provided files.
remediation.suggestion: Add a shared safe extraction helper that rejects absolute paths, parent traversal, unsafe symlinks, and oversized archive members before writing files.

remediation.issue: Formula recalculation installs a persistent LibreOffice macro in the user profile.
remediation.suggestion: Use a temporary isolated LibreOffice user profile for recalculation, or clearly prompt before writing persistent macro files under the home directory.

remediation.issue: The tooling invokes external commands for LibreOffice, gcc, git diff, and timeout handling.
remediation.suggestion: Keep command arguments fixed, avoid shell execution, document required binaries, and minimize inherited environment variables for subprocess calls.

static_findings.snippet: - Removes rsid attributes from runs (revision metadata that doesn't affect rendering)
return None, f"Error: {input_file} is not a valid Office file"
- A packed Office file (.docx/.pptx/.xlsx) which will be unpacked to a temp directory
help="Path to unpacked directory or packed Office file (.docx/.pptx/.xlsx)",
help="Path to original file (.docx/.pptx/.xlsx). If omitted, all XSD errors are reported and redlini
f"Error: Cannot determine file type from {path}. Use --original or provide a .docx/.pptx/.xlsx file.
"PASSED - All references are valid and all files are properly referenced"
f"Line {elem.sourceline}: ID '{value}' appears to be a UUID but contains invalid hex characters"
print("PASSED - All UUID-like IDs contain valid hex values")
if r_id and r_id not in valid_layout_rids:
"Remove invalid references or add missing slide layouts to the relationships file."
print("PASSED - All slide layout IDs reference valid slide layouts")
**LibreOffice Required for Formula Recalculation**: You can assume LibreOffice is installed for reca
- If `status` is `errors_found`, check `error_summary` for specific error types and locations
Excel files created or modified by openpyxl contain formulas as strings but not calculated values. U
- [ ] **NaN handling**: Check for null values with `pd.notna()`
- [ ] **Division by zero**: Check denominators before using `/` in formulas (#DIV/0!)
- Use `data_only=True` to read calculated values: `load_workbook('file.xlsx', data_only=True)`
- **Warning**: If opened with `data_only=True` and saved, formulas are replaced with values and perm
- For large files: Use `read_only=True` for reading or `write_only=True` for writing
- Specify data types to avoid inference issues: `pd.read_excel('file.xlsx', dtype={'id': str})`
- For large files, read specific columns: `pd.read_excel('file.xlsx', usecols=['A', 'C', 'E'])`
- Specify data types to avoid inference issues: `pd.read_excel('file.xlsx', dtype={'id': str})`
- Avoid verbose variable names and redundant operations

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet work often needs reliable formulas, formatting, and file output. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Excel creation, editing, validation, and conversion workflows.
actual_capabilities: Creates new XLSX files from tabular data or user requirements.
Edits existing XLSX, XLSM, CSV, and TSV files while preserving useful workbook structure.
Uses pandas for data cleaning, analysis, restructuring, and bulk spreadsheet operations.
Uses openpyxl for formulas, cell formatting, workbook structure, and Excel-specific features.
Runs provided LibreOffice-based tooling to recalculate formulas and detect Excel formula errors.
Supports Office archive unpacking, validation, and repacking workflows for advanced file repair.
limitations: Formula recalculation depends on LibreOffice being installed and callable as soffice.
The skill does not integrate with the Google Sheets API or cloud spreadsheet services.
Very large workbooks may need read-only, write-only, or chunked processing strategies.
It cannot guarantee compatibility with every Excel feature outside the supported Python libraries.

use_cases.title: Clean operational exports
use_cases.description: Turn messy CSV, TSV, or Excel exports into structured workbooks with consistent columns and formatting.

use_cases.title: Build financial models
use_cases.description: Create Excel models with formulas, assumption cells, professional number formats, and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Repair workbook issues
use_cases.description: Inspect formulas, detect common Excel errors, and produce a corrected workbook file for review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an XLSX file from this data. Add clear headers, freeze the top row, and format numbers for easy review.

prompt_templates.title: Clean an Existing Spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the attached spreadsheet, clean inconsistent rows, standardize column names, and save a corrected XLSX file.

prompt_templates.title: Add Formulas and Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this workbook with formulas, totals, percentage calculations, and professional formatting. Recalculate and check for formula errors.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Complex Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this workbook for broken formulas, invalid references, formatting issues, and data type problems. Return a fixed spreadsheet file.

output_examples.input: A sales CSV with inconsistent headers, blank rows, and mixed date formats.
output_examples.output: A cleaned XLSX workbook with normalized headers, valid dates, formatted currency columns, and a summary worksheet.

output_examples.input: An existing Excel model that needs new revenue assumptions and margin calculations.
output_examples.output: An updated workbook with linked assumption cells, dynamic formulas, consistent number formats, and no detected formula errors.

output_examples.input: A spreadsheet with suspected broken references after rows were inserted.
output_examples.output: A repaired workbook and a concise explanation of formula errors found and corrected.

best_practices: Use formulas for workbook calculations so results update when source data changes.
Preserve existing workbook styles and structure unless the user requests a redesign.
Recalculate and verify workbooks before delivery when formulas are added or changed.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode calculated results when an Excel formula should remain dynamic.
Do not save a workbook opened with calculated values only if formulas must be preserved.
Do not use this skill when the deliverable is a report, script, or cloud integration instead of a spreadsheet file.

faq.question: Which file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports XLSX, XLSM, CSV, and TSV workflows where the final deliverable is a spreadsheet file.

faq.question: Can it create spreadsheets from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create new workbooks with sheets, formulas, formatting, charts, and cleaned tabular data.

faq.question: Can it preserve formulas in existing workbooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends openpyxl workflows that preserve formulas, styles, sheets, and workbook structure.

faq.question: Does formula recalculation require extra software?
faq.answer: Yes. The included recalculation workflow requires LibreOffice to be installed and available as soffice.

faq.question: Can it fix messy CSV files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can clean malformed rows, headers, data types, dates, and columns before exporting to XLSX.

faq.question: Does it connect to Google Sheets?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on local spreadsheet files and does not provide Google Sheets API integration.
